critic_raw: Four issues: preprocessing leakage, temporal split violation, metric inconsistency between hypothesis and evaluation section, and feature asymmetry confounding architectural attribution.
all_issues_raised: Preprocessing transformers fit on entire dataset before splitting — data leakage
Stratified random split on longitudinal diabetic retinopathy data ignores temporal ordering — causal inversion
Hypothesis specifies AUC as primary metric but Section 5 uses Macro-Averaged F1 — internal metric inconsistency
CNN uses image + EHR features; baseline uses only EHR — cannot isolate deep learning architecture contribution from imaging modality advantage

round_transcripts.critic: Four issues: preprocessing leakage, temporal split violation, metric inconsistency between hypothesis and evaluation section, and feature asymmetry confounding architectural attribution.
round_transcripts.defender:
**On Issue 1 (Preprocessing leakage):** Confirmed — full-dataset fitting is explicit in the design.

**On Issue 2 (Temporal split):** Confirmed — random split on longitudinal progression data violates causal ordering.

**On Issue 3 (Metric inconsistency):** This is a real inconsistency. The hypothesis says 'measured by AUC' and the evaluation section specifies Macro-Averaged F1 as primary. These are different metrics with different properties (AUC is threshold-independent; F1 requires threshold specification). Model selection on F1 and hypothesis testing on AUC means the model optimized for one metric is evaluated against a different criterion. This is confirmed as a design inconsistency.

**On Issue 4 (Feature asymmetry):** CNN uses image + tabular; baseline uses only tabular. Any improvement is attributable to either (a) imaging data or (b) CNN architecture applied to combined data. These cannot be disentangled without an additional ablation (e.g., traditional model + image features). This is confirmed as a valid confound.

round_transcripts.critic: All four issues confirmed by the defense. All remain unresolved.
round_transcripts.defender: All four issues stand. Issue 3 (metric inconsistency) and Issue 4 (feature asymmetry) are the most material for scientific interpretation.

all_issues_adjudicated: Preprocessing transformers fit on entire dataset before splitting — data leakage
Stratified random split on longitudinal data ignores temporal ordering
Hypothesis specifies AUC but evaluation section uses Macro-Averaged F1 — metric inconsistency
CNN uses image + EHR features; baseline uses only EHR — feature asymmetry confounds architectural comparison
